# Test fixtures — `signing` module

**Last updated:** 2026-05-11 (round-1 answers integrated)
**Status:** **Round-1 signed off 2026-05-11.** Paired with `signing-api.md` (round-1 answers integrated 2026-05-11) per T-005. Module #5 of 6.

---

## Round-1 review answers (2026-05-11)

| # | Topic | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Input-binary content realism | **(a) synthetic** random-byte `.bin`s for F-IN-* substrate-level fixtures. Real binaries belong in hardware-layer F-SS-* (user has N6 hw per Q4). |
| Q2 | F-OUT-* outcome fixtures | **(b) all recorded.** User forces every failure path on the host for verbatim captures. Diverges from my (a) mixed proposal. Note: per signing-api.md Q2 ratified (c) skip device-family check, F-OUT-DEVICE-FAMILY-REJECTED is dropped — substrate no longer rejects by family. Remaining 3 fixtures (SUCCESS / ALIGN-REQUIRED / CLI-FAILED) all recorded. |
| Q3 | Per-image_type size variants | **(c) single shared `generic.bin`.** Collapse F-IN-FSBL/SSBL/COPRO into one shared `tests/fixtures/signing/inputs/generic.bin`; tests parametrize image_type against the same input. Simplifies authoring (one file vs three). |
| Q4 | Hardware fixtures defer-vs-skeleton | **Real N6/MP hardware available** + multiple hw platforms. F-SS-* fixtures **in scope for v1**, not deferred. F-PROJ-NUCLEO-N657X0-Q-SIGNED reference project also in scope. Cross-family hardware coverage achievable. Saved as memory: `project_substrate_hardware_bench.md` — applies to ALL N6-only features across the substrate (cubeprogrammer F-006/F-009/F-010 + F-021, debug F-SVDX-H7/U5/N6, compound F-015). |

Inline question text + per-Q resolution lines preserved below for audit.
**Scope:** test inputs the signing module's tests need across unit / smoke / hardware layers. Smallest fixture catalog in the substrate (one prompt = small surface).
**Build status:** spec only. Fixture artifacts supplied by the user during the code phase; tests gracefully skip on `[out]` items until artifacts arrive.

---

## How this fixture catalog works

(Same model as cubeide / debug / vcp fixture-specs ratified per RES-011 / RES-013 / RES-014.)

Each fixture requirement has: **ID** · **Status** (`[in]`/`[out]`) · **Description** · **Features required** · **Drop path** · **Multi-artifact** (yes — supply variants for broader coverage) · **Drives tests**.

Tests parametrize over the artifacts under the requirement's drop path; empty dir → `pytest.skip` with the path to populate.

**v1 fixture-authoring rules** (per M-020):
- LF line endings on all text files (signing-tool stdout captures, descriptors).
- Anonymized addresses / paths where artifacts come from real user projects.

---

| Group | Count | Path prefix | Used by |
|---|---|---|---|
| Unsigned input binary (single shared per Q3) | 1 (`generic.bin`) | `tests/fixtures/signing/inputs/F-IN-GENERIC/` | substrate unit + smoke |
| SigningTool stdout/stderr capture fixtures (Q2: all recorded; minus DEVICE-FAMILY-REJECTED which Q2(c) dropped) | 5 | `tests/fixtures/signing/outcomes/F-OUT-*/` | substrate unit (outcome detection per substrate-captures rule). Per RES-020: SUCCESS + ALIGN-REQUIRED + UNSUPPORTED-FAMILY + COPRO-NO-ENTRY-POINT + CLI-FAILED. |
| Descriptor fixtures (substrate-authored) — slimmed since `firmware.device_family` not used | 1 | `descriptors/` | substrate unit |
| Expected-signed-binary samples (in scope per Q4 — user has N6 hw) | 1+ | `tests/fixtures/signing/signed-samples/F-SS-*/` | hardware (round-trip via cubeprogrammer flash_signed) |
| N6 reference project (in scope per Q4) | 1 | `tests/fixtures/projects/F-PROJ-NUCLEO-N657X0-Q-SIGNED/` | hardware (cross-module F-015 compound) |

## Unsigned input binary (F-IN-GENERIC) — single shared file per Q3 ratified

Small `.bin` file used as input to `sign_binary()`. Substrate doesn't parse content; SigningTool consumes the bytes. Per Q3 ratified 2026-05-11, **one shared file** covers fsbl / ssbl / copro tests (parametrized by `image_type` kwarg) — collapses the earlier F-IN-FSBL / F-IN-SSBL / F-IN-COPRO breakout.

### F-IN-GENERIC — Synthetic input binary (substrate-authored)

**Status:** `[in]` — **generated by the test harness on first run**, not user-supplied. The content is content-irrelevant (substrate doesn't inspect, SigningTool just consumes bytes), so a deterministic random-byte fixture produced at test time removes a manual setup step and removes the "user-supplied" bottleneck for substrate unit tests.
**Path:** `tests/fixtures/signing/inputs/F-IN-GENERIC/`
**Description:** A small (~16 KB) deterministic random-byte `.bin` (seed fixed in test harness, e.g., `random.Random(0xC0DE)`).

**Features required:**
- File size in the few-KB to ~32 KB range (typical FSBL/SSBL/copro sizes).
- Filename `generic.bin`.
- Deterministic content (seeded RNG; reproducible across hosts).
- LF line endings on any sidecar text files (per M-020).

**Multi-artifact:** **single file only per Q3 ratified.** Tests parametrize against this one input with different `image_type=` / `header_version=` kwargs.

**Drives tests:**
- `sign_binary(input=generic.bin, image_type="fsbl", header_version="2", ...)` — fsbl path.
- `sign_binary(input=generic.bin, image_type="ssbl", header_version="2.3", ...)` — ssbl path + auto-align (with `device_family="STM32N6XX"`).
- `sign_binary(input=generic.bin, image_type="copro", header_version="2", ...)` — copro path (no entry_point requirement).
- Output file size > input file size (signed binaries add header).

---

## SigningTool stdout/stderr capture fixtures (F-OUT-*) — Q2 ratified all-recorded

Captured SigningTool invocations for outcome-detection tests. Per substrate-captures-doesn't-interpret, substrate doesn't parse content; it just checks exit code + captures the log. Each fixture is a (stdout.txt, stderr.txt, exit_code.txt) triple, plus a JSON sidecar describing the expected `SigningResult` (or expected exception).

**Per Q2 ratified 2026-05-11: all fixtures are recorded captures.** User forces each failure path on the host to produce verbatim outputs. **Per Q2 in signing-api.md (skip device-family check)**, the previously-proposed `F-OUT-DEVICE-FAMILY-REJECTED` fixture is **dropped** — substrate no longer pre-rejects by family, so there's no substrate-side rejection path to test. If the user invokes against an unsupported family, SigningTool itself fails → captured as F-OUT-CLI-FAILED variant (or the dedicated `F-OUT-UNSUPPORTED-FAMILY` per RES-018).

**`expected.json` field-comparison rules** (avoids brittle equality on host/tool-version-dependent values):

| Field | Comparison |
|---|---|
| `input_path`, `output_path`, `load_address`, `entry_point`, `image_type`, `header_version`, `option_flags`, `no_auth_flag`, `align_applied`, `device_family` | exact equality |
| `bytes_in` | exact equality (input is fixture-controlled) |
| `bytes_out` | predicate: `bytes_out > bytes_in` (signed binary adds header; exact size is tool-version-dependent) |
| `log_path` | predicate: `.exists()` (substrate writes a real file per invocation; path varies) |
| `duration_s` | predicate: `duration_s >= 0.0` (host-dependent) |
| Error: `signing_marker`, `recoverable`, `input_path`, `device_family`, `header_version` | exact equality |
| Error: `message` | predicate: substring match against captured stderr (vendor wording may drift across tool versions) |

Test code provides a `compare_signing_result(observed, expected_json)` helper that applies these rules.

### F-OUT-SUCCESS — Successful sign

**Status:** `[out]`
**Path:** `tests/fixtures/signing/outcomes/F-OUT-SUCCESS/`
**Description:** Recorded capture from a real successful `STM32_SigningTool_CLI` invocation on the user's N6 host. Exit 0; banner + progress lines.

**Features required:**
- `stdout.txt` — verbatim tool stdout.
- `stderr.txt` — usually empty for success.
- `exit_code.txt` — `0`.
- `expected.json` — `SigningResult` shape with the params used to produce the capture.

**Multi-artifact:** yes — different header versions (hv=2, hv=2.3) + different image types. Each variant is one recorded capture.

**Drives tests:** `sign_binary()` happy path → `SigningResult.duration_s` measured, `log_path` exists and matches the capture.

### F-OUT-ALIGN-REQUIRED — Vendor `--align`-required failure (reference-only / test-via-no-device-family path)

**Status:** `[out]`
**Path:** `tests/fixtures/signing/outcomes/F-OUT-ALIGN-REQUIRED/`
**Description:** **Recorded capture** from invoking SigningTool with `-hv 2.3` on real N6 hardware WITHOUT `--align`. Tool prints alignment-error message; exit non-zero.

**Features required:**
- `stdout.txt` / `stderr.txt` — verbatim tool error output.
- `exit_code.txt` — non-zero.
- `expected.json` — `SigningToolError(signing_marker="signing-cli-failed")` (the vendor-error capture path, NOT `"align-required"`).

**Test path it drives:** the `device_family=None` path — caller doesn't know the family, substrate doesn't auto-set `--align`, SigningTool fails with its alignment-error stderr. Substrate captures verbatim as `signing-cli-failed`. Validates the parse path against real vendor stderr.

**Not driving:** the pre-flight `signing_marker="align-required"` raise (which happens when caller passes `device_family="STM32N6XX"` + `align=False` explicitly) — that path has no subprocess so this fixture isn't reached. Substrate's pre-flight check is exercised by a separate substrate-unit test with no fixture (synthetic kwargs, expected raise; ValueError-style).

### F-OUT-UNSUPPORTED-FAMILY — SigningTool rejects non-N6/MP\* family (recorded)

**Status:** `[out]`
**Path:** `tests/fixtures/signing/outcomes/F-OUT-UNSUPPORTED-FAMILY/`
**Description:** **Recorded capture** from invoking SigningTool against an unsupported family (e.g., a generic L4 binary with `image_type="fsbl"`). SigningTool itself rejects with its family-restriction error message; exit non-zero. Pairs with the substrate principle ratified in RES-018 — neither cubeprogrammer nor signing pre-rejects; vendor error surfaces verbatim.

**Features required:**
- `stdout.txt` / `stderr.txt` — verbatim tool error output mentioning unsupported family / image-type combo.
- `exit_code.txt` — non-zero.
- `expected.json` — `SigningToolError(signing_marker="signing-cli-failed")` with `log_path` populated.

**Drives tests:** substrate proceeds without family pre-check (per RES-015 Q2(c) and RES-018); vendor error captured; caller sees `signing-cli-failed` + raw stderr.

### F-OUT-COPRO-NO-ENTRY-POINT — copro path without entry_point (substrate-authored, happy)

**Status:** `[out]`
**Path:** `tests/fixtures/signing/outcomes/F-OUT-COPRO-NO-ENTRY-POINT/`
**Description:** Successful sign of `F-IN-GENERIC` with `image_type="copro"` and `entry_point=None` (omitted). Substrate's conditional-validation rule (entry_point required iff `image_type ∈ {fsbl, ssbl}`) lets this through; the SigningTool argv omits `-ep`. Locks down the conditional-required behavior end-to-end.

**Features required:**
- `stdout.txt` / `stderr.txt` — captured.
- `exit_code.txt` — `0`.
- `expected.json` — `SigningResult` with `entry_point=None`.

**Drives tests:** conditional-validation pass-through; substrate doesn't emit `-ep` when entry_point is None and image_type is copro/teeh/teed/teex.

### F-OUT-CLI-FAILED — Subprocess non-zero exit (recorded)

**Status:** `[out]`
**Path:** `tests/fixtures/signing/outcomes/F-OUT-CLI-FAILED/`
**Description:** **Recorded capture** (per Q2 ratified all-recorded) from a SigningTool invocation that exits non-zero after substrate's pre-checks passed. Easy to force with intentionally-bad params (e.g., malformed `-of` value, missing `-ep` for fsbl, unsupported device family).

**Features required:**
- `stdout.txt` / `stderr.txt` — captured.
- `exit_code.txt` — non-zero.
- `expected.json` — `SigningToolError(signing_marker="signing-cli-failed")` with `log_path` pointing at the captured log.

**Multi-artifact:** yes — variants per failure cause (bad option flags / unsupported family / etc.). The unsupported-family case is the most useful: validates that substrate's Q2(c) "skip the check" lets SigningTool's own family error surface verbatim.

**Drives tests:** substrate captures the failure; raises with `log_path`; verbatim tool output preserved for caller (Claude / cubeprogrammer's flash_signed_pair) to inspect.

---

## Descriptor fixtures (substrate-authored)

Slimmed per signing-api.md Q2 ratified — substrate doesn't read `firmware.device_family` (no descriptor-field fallback). One baseline descriptor for resolution tests:

| File | Drives |
|---|---|
| `descriptors/signing-baseline.jsonc` | Baseline `stm32-project.jsonc` for signing tests. No `firmware.device_family` field (per Q2 ratified — substrate doesn't read it). Used as resolution-test input. |

**Dropped from initial draft** (per Q2 ratified 2026-05-11): `signing-n6.jsonc`, `signing-mp1.jsonc`, `signing-unsupported.jsonc` — substrate no longer consults the descriptor for device-family info.

## Expected-signed-binary samples (F-SS-*) — in scope per Q4 ratified

**Per Q4 ratified 2026-05-11**, user has N6/MP/MP2 hardware on the bench. Hardware tests are in scope for v1; no longer deferred.

### F-SS-N6-FSBL-HV2.3 — Real signed-binary sample

**Status:** `[out]` (planning artifact; user supplies during code phase from real N6 sign invocation).
**Path:** `tests/fixtures/signing/signed-samples/F-SS-N6-FSBL-HV2.3/`
**Description:** Captured output of a real `sign_binary()` invocation against a real N6 input. Drives the hardware test that flashes the signed binary via `cubeprogrammer.flash_signed()` and verifies the chip boots.

**Features required:**
- The trusted-binary file (output of a successful `sign_binary()` call).
- Sidecar `params.json` documenting the input + params used.
- LF line endings on text sidecars.

**Multi-artifact:** yes — variants per header_version (hv=2, hv=2.3) and per image_type (fsbl, ssbl, copro).

**Drives tests:** hardware-layer cross-module compound (signing.sign_binary → cubeprogrammer.flash_signed → cubeprogrammer.connect-and-verify-boot).

### F-PROJ-NUCLEO-N657X0-Q-SIGNED — N6 reference project (shared per T-004)

**Status:** `[out]` (planning artifact; user supplies during code phase).
**Path:** `tests/fixtures/projects/F-PROJ-NUCLEO-N657X0-Q-SIGNED/`
**Description:** A real N6 reference project that compiles to an FSBL/SSBL pair suitable for signing + flashing to N6 hardware. Shared with `compound/n6_flash_boot.py` (F-015).

**Features required:**
- Builds clean on user's host via cubeide.
- Produces .bin output suitable as input to `sign_binary()`.
- Includes a known-good config matching the canonical N6 dev board on user's bench.

**Drives tests:** F-015 compound (build → sign → flash_external → boot-verify).

---

## Layer breakdown

### Unit-layer (T-001) — primary coverage

- Validation tests (address regex, device-family prefix check, image_type/entry_point cross-check, header_version/align auto-resolution, output-exists refusal).
- Outcome-detection tests against F-OUT-* fixtures: success / device-rejected / align-required / cli-failed.
- Default output-path computation (input=`app.bin` → output=`app-trusted.bin`).
- Descriptor fallback: `device_family=None` + descriptor → resolves; both None → loud error.
- Subprocess mocked; tests don't invoke real SigningTool.

### Smoke-layer (T-002) — real CLI, no hardware

- `STM32_SigningTool_CLI -h` reachable.
- `sign_binary()` invocation against F-IN-FSBL with valid N6 params → produces an output file. Substrate doesn't parse the content; assertion is "file exists + size > input size".
- Version probe (TODO per Q7 round-2): if a known-good version cutoff exists, warn on older.

### Hardware-layer (T-003) — N6/MP/MP2 hardware required

- **Deferred entirely until N6 hardware on the bench.** v1 with NUCLEO-L476RG cannot exercise SigningTool meaningfully (the canonical hardware is unsupported by SigningTool per UM2543 Table 1).
- When N6 hardware lands: cross-module compound (signing → cubeprogrammer.flash_signed → cubeprogrammer.connect + boot verify).

### Eval-layer (T-007) — placeholder

No T3 prompts in this module. No eval fixtures.

---

## Cross-tool sharing

| Shared fixture | Owners |
|---|---|
| `tests/fixtures/projects/F-PROJ-NUCLEO-N657X0-Q-SIGNED/` (TODO when N6 hw) | signing (build + sign owner), cubeprogrammer (flash_signed), compound (F-015 N6 boot-from-flash) |

---

## Build sequence

Per T-005:

1. **API-surface phase (now):** spec only.
2. **Code phase (incremental as user supplies):**
   - **User** generates F-IN-* binaries — small `.bin` files matching FSBL / SSBL / coprocessor shape. Can be anything plausible; substrate doesn't validate content.
   - **User** captures F-OUT-* by running `STM32_SigningTool_CLI` with deliberate params (real successes for F-OUT-SUCCESS; deliberately-bad params for F-OUT-CLI-FAILED; pre-subprocess synthesized for F-OUT-DEVICE-FAMILY-REJECTED).
   - **Substrate-authored** descriptors by Claude.
   - Tests run continuously against whatever's supplied; missing fixtures cleanly skip.
3. **Hardware phase:** deferred until N6 hardware on the bench. When it lands, capture F-SS-* + add F-PROJ-NUCLEO-N657X0-Q-SIGNED reference project.

---

## Round-1 review questions

Per the inline-explanation discipline. The smallest module also has the smallest set of fixture-spec questions — 4.

---

### Q1. Input-binary content realism — real FSBL/SSBL vs synthetic

**Context.** F-IN-* fixtures are input `.bin` files for `sign_binary()`. Substrate doesn't inspect their content; SigningTool does. Authoring options:

- **(a) Synthetic** — small random-byte `.bin` files of plausible sizes (proposal). Cheap to author; substrate's tests don't care about the content.
- **(b) Real FSBL/SSBL/copro binaries** from a user-built project. More realistic; cumbersome to author (requires N6 dev setup).

**Trade-off.** (a) is fine for substrate-level tests (validation, subprocess invocation, outcome detection). (b) is overkill for substrate; real-FSBL tests belong in hardware-layer F-SS-* fixtures.

**Proposal:** (a) synthetic random-byte `.bin`s for F-IN-* substrate-level fixtures. **TODO(hardware-phase):** F-SS-* gets real binaries when N6 hardware lands.

**Pick.**

**Resolved (2026-05-11):** (a) synthetic random-byte F-IN-* (substrate-unit-test inputs). Real-binary tests live in hardware-layer F-SS-* (in scope per Q4).

---

### Q2. F-OUT-* outcome fixtures — recorded vs synthesized

**Context.** F-OUT-* fixtures capture SigningTool stdout/stderr/exit_code triples. Authoring options:

- **(a) Recorded** for F-OUT-SUCCESS + F-OUT-CLI-FAILED (real captures from running the tool on the user's host); **synthesized** for F-OUT-DEVICE-FAMILY-REJECTED (substrate-side, no tool involvement) + F-OUT-ALIGN-REQUIRED (recordable but easier to synthesize).
- **(b) All recorded** — requires forcing each failure path on the user's host.
- **(c) All synthesized** — easy to author but diverges from real tool output.

**Trade-off.** (a) mixed gets realism where it matters (substrate's outcome-detection consumes the tool's output verbatim) without forcing the user to force every failure. (b) is heavy. (c) misses real-tool behavior.

**Proposal:** (a) mixed strategy. Pick.

**Resolved (2026-05-11):** **(b) all recorded.** User forces every failure path on the bench. Combined with signing-api.md Q2(c) "skip device-family check", the previously-proposed F-OUT-DEVICE-FAMILY-REJECTED fixture is dropped — substrate no longer pre-rejects. Three F-OUT-* fixtures remain (SUCCESS / ALIGN-REQUIRED / CLI-FAILED), all recorded.

---

### Q3. Synthetic input-binary size variants

**Context.** F-IN-FSBL proposes a `~16 KB` default + a `~128 KB` larger variant. Question: how many size variants per image_type?

- **(a) One per image_type** (proposal). Cheapest authoring; covers the substrate's contract (it doesn't care about size beyond bytes-in / bytes-out reporting).
- **(b) Two per image_type** (small + large) — covers any size-dependent behavior in SigningTool itself.
- **(c) None** — single shared `tests/fixtures/signing/inputs/generic.bin` covering all image_types. Tests parametrize.

**Trade-off.** (a) is reasonable middle. (b) overkill for v1. (c) loses image-type-specific naming clarity.

**Proposal:** (a) one per image_type (fsbl + ssbl + copro = 3 files). Pick.

**Resolved (2026-05-11): (c) single shared `generic.bin`.** No per-image_type variants. Tests parametrize image_type / header_version against the one input. F-IN-FSBL / F-IN-SSBL / F-IN-COPRO collapsed into F-IN-GENERIC.

---

### Q4. Hardware fixtures (F-SS-*) — defer or skeleton-out now?

**Context.** F-SS-* fixtures need real N6 hardware. The user's canonical hardware (NUCLEO-L476RG) is unsupported by SigningTool. Two options:

- **(a) Defer entirely** (proposal). Mark F-SS-N6-FSBL-HV2.3 as `[out]` with a clear "deferred until N6 hardware" annotation. Don't author anything in v1.
- **(b) Skeleton-out now** — author the directory structure + an `expected.json` even without the real binary, so the fixture is ready when N6 hardware lands.

**Trade-off.** (a) is M-018 simple-now — author what you can run; defer what you can't. (b) gives a starting point but creates clutter.

**Proposal:** (a) defer entirely. The catalog table above lists F-SS-N6-FSBL-HV2.3 as a planning placeholder; no on-disk fixture in v1.

**Pick.**

**Resolved (2026-05-11):** **Real N6/MP hardware available** + multiple hw platforms — user direction confirms hardware tests in scope for v1, not deferred. F-SS-N6-FSBL-HV2.3 + F-PROJ-NUCLEO-N657X0-Q-SIGNED + cross-family hardware coverage all in scope. **Saved as memory** (`project_substrate_hardware_bench.md`) — the user-has-N6-hw fact applies broadly across the substrate: cubeprogrammer's F-006/F-009/F-010 + F-021, debug's F-SVDX-H7/U5/N6, compound F-015. Already-signed-off fixture-specs stay as-is (status `[out]` per user-provides model); user supplies artifacts when ready. **Round-2 cross-module candidate flagged**: a single fixture-spec refresh pass to drop "deferred without N6 hw" annotations across modules.

---

- **Round-1 review answers integrated 2026-05-11.** All 4 questions resolved (see "Round-1 review answers" at top + per-question audit above).
- **Build deferred** to code phase per T-005.
- **Signing module fully signed off** — both `signing-api.md` and this fixture-spec ratified. Module #5 of 6 complete.
- **v1 in-scope substrate-unit-test fixtures**: F-IN-GENERIC + F-OUT-SUCCESS + F-OUT-ALIGN-REQUIRED + F-OUT-CLI-FAILED + descriptors/signing-baseline.jsonc.
- **v1 in-scope hardware-test fixtures**: F-SS-N6-FSBL-HV2.3 + F-PROJ-NUCLEO-N657X0-Q-SIGNED + cross-family variants (user supplies during code phase against real N6/MP/MP2 hw).
- **Next module after sign-off:** `cubemx` (MX-* + async-completion per SB-004) — the final module per M-015 step 2.
